channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Pakistan reaffirms stance against Houthi attacks on maritime assets
item.description: Pakistan has issued a firm warning to Yemen's Houthi rebels, stating that any attack on its maritime interests will be treated as an act of aggression against the country. Foreign Office spokesperson ...

item.title: Sophie Thatcher Reflects on Leaving 'Yellowjackets' and Her Future Roles
item.description: Sophie Thatcher, 25, has confirmed she is leaving the Paramount+ series 'Yellowjackets' after its fourth and final season. In an interview with Variety, she expressed that portraying teenage Natalie S...

item.title: BOSA terminates Ayanda Allie's membership after disciplinary process
item.description: The Board of Build One South Africa (BOSA) has officially terminated the party membership of Ayanda Allie, a member of the Gauteng Provincial Legislature. This decision followed a prolonged disciplina...

item.title: Arrest of Mamasapano Massacre Suspect
item.description: A former member of the Bangsamoro Islamic Freedom Fighters-Dawlah Islamiya (BIFF-DI), implicated in the 2015 massacre of 44 Philippine National Police Special Action Force (SAF) commandos, was arreste...

item.title: Tragic mining accident in Queensland leaves one worker dead and another critically injured
item.description: A fatal workplace incident occurred at the BMA-owned Peak Downs coal mine near Moranbah, central Queensland. A light vehicle was struck by a large mining truck around 4:30am on Friday, resulting in th...

item.title: Delhi University Advises Students to Avoid Protests at Jantar Mantar Amid Legal Concerns
item.description: The University of Delhi has issued a social media appeal urging students and faculty to avoid participating in the ongoing CJP-led protest at Jantar Mantar, citing safety risks and potential legal con...

item.title: Contrasting Approaches to Tobacco and Opioid Regulation: A Call for Coherence in Harm Reduction Strategies
item.description: The article by Chris Selley critiques the inconsistency in public health policies regarding addiction. While tobacco stigmatization and restrictive measures have successfully reduced smoking rates ove...

item.title: NDC Candidate Calls for Transparent Tax Enforcement in Kwara
item.description: Prof. Abdulmumin Yinka Ajia, NDC's gubernatorial candidate in Kwara State, has criticized the Kwara State Internal Revenue Service (KW IRS) for using heavy-handed tax enforcement measures. He demanded...

item.title: PIC governance crisis escalates as Masondo resigns amid board resignations and BEE financing probe
item.description: Deputy Finance Minister David Masondo has resigned as chairperson of the Public Investment Corporation (PIC) board, citing the need to safeguard the stability of the state asset manager and maintain p...

item.title: Supreme Court allows industries in Taj zone with environmental safeguards
item.description: The Supreme Court (SC) has lifted a two-year-old blanket ban on establishing industries in the Taj Trapezium Zone (TTZ), a sensitive area near the Taj Mahal. This decision opens avenues for employment...

item.title: Toronto Mayoral Candidate Proposes Hiring 1,000 New Police Officers
item.description: Toronto mayoral candidate Brad Bradford has proposed hiring 1,000 additional police officers if elected, arguing that the city's current five-year hiring plan is insufficient to address population gro...

item.title: Abuja Police Take Over Probe Into Mary Habila's Death Amid Alleged Political Tensions
item.description: The police in Abuja have taken over the investigation into the death of Mary Habila, a young woman who died at the residence of Nigeria's Works Minister, Senator David Umahi. This development comes af...

item.title: Exploring Communalism Through 10 Conversations
item.description: The article presents a dialogue between a guru and a disciple, exploring the theme of communalism. The guru critiques Muslims for their perceived role in societal division, questioning their loyalty a...

item.title: Chinese Coast Guard Engages in Water Cannon Incident Near Philippine Vessel in Bajo de Masinloc
item.description: The Chinese Coast Guard (CCG) engaged in a tense standoff with a Philippine vessel in the West Philippine Sea, specifically near Bajo de Masinloc. According to reports from GMA News, the CCG vessel tr...

item.title: The 2020s: A Decade of Innovation in Cinema
item.description: The article explores how the 2020s transformed cinema through technological advancements and cultural shifts. Key innovations include YouTube's influence on filmmaking, with directors like Curry Barke...

item.title: IBM Reports Earnings and Stock Performance
item.description: IBM (NYSE: IBM) reported its quarterly earnings, revealing a mixed financial performance. The company's revenue grew by 3% year-over-year, reaching $16.2 billion, while net income increased by 5% to $...

item.title: Trump faces backlash for pushing unpopular data center initiatives despite community resistance
item.description: U.S. President Donald Trump faced criticism from both supporters and opponents after advocating for communities to accept data centers, which have become a contentious topic due to environmental conce...

item.title: Activist criticizes Rahul Gandhi's protest as lacking sincerity
item.description: Gitanjali J Angmo, wife of activist Sonam Wangchuk, has condemned the flash protest led by Congress leader Rahul Gandhi near Prime Minister Narendra Modi's residence as 'insincere'. In an interview wi...

item.title: Landfill Fire Continues to Burn Near Canberra; Health Advisories Issued
item.description: A landfill fire near Canberra, specifically at the Canberra Construction Recyclers site on Pialligo Avenue, remains active despite efforts by emergency services. The blaze was reported late on July 23...

item.title: Queensland records first suspected bird flu case after dead petrel found on Moreton Island
item.description: Queensland has recorded its first suspected case of H5 high pathogenicity avian influenza after a deceased petrel washed up on Moreton Island tested positive during initial screening. The sample is no...
